cves.cwe: CWE-327 (Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm)
cves.description: python-jose through 3.3.0 has algorithm confusion with OpenSSH ECDSA keys and other key formats. Similar to CVE-2022-29217.

python-jose through 3.3.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (resource consumption) during a decode via a crafted JSON Web Encryption (JWE) token with a high compression ratio, aka a 'JWT bomb'. Similar to CVE-2024-21319.

cves.type: Timing Side-Channel Attack (Transitive via ecdsa dependency)
cves.description: The `ecdsa` PyPI package versions 0.18.0 and prior are vulnerable to the Minerva timing attack. As of time of publication, no known patched version exists.

open_security_issues.title: Security issue: Minerva timing attack vulnerability in python-ecdsa usage
Security: DER key algorithm confusion, empty HMAC key, and timing side-channels

recommended_replacements.notes: JWT-only implementation. API is similar to python-jose. Backed by Auth0. Most popular Python JWT library.
Full JOSE stack: JWS, JWE, JWK, JWA, JWT per RFCs 7515-7519. Most comprehensive and RFC-compliant.

project_impact.usage_pattern: JWT encode/decode with HS256 (internal tokens) and RS256 (Azure AD JWKS validation)
project_impact.direct_cve_risk: CVE-2024-33663 affects ECDSA keys; project uses HS256/RS256 so direct exploitation is limited. However, library supply chain is compromised.
project_impact.recommendation: Migrate to PyJWT 2.12.1 — lowest effort, highest community support, backed by Auth0
